---
title: "combine_forecast_obs_WOW"
author: "Wenkai_Zhong"
date: "2025-07-25"
output: html_document
---

############################### before run this file u should run three individual data import files first (forecast,obs,wow)#######################################
#Visualise all offical stations, cities and wow stations
```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(dplyr)
library(maps)
library(mapdata)

# Create the combined map
gridpoint_station_map <- ggplot() +
  # Add Netherlands boundary using maps package
  borders("world", regions = "Netherlands", 
          fill = NA, colour = "black", size = 0.8) +
  
  # Connection lines between gridpoints and stations
  geom_segment(data = gridpoint_station_table,
               aes(x = gp_lon, y = gp_lat, xend = sta_lon, yend = sta_lat),
               alpha = 0.3) +
  
  # Plot gridpoint points (dark blue squares)
  geom_point(data = gridpoint_station_table,
             aes(x = gp_lon, y = gp_lat, shape = "Gridpoints"),
             color = "darkblue", size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
  
  # Plot primary stations (red circles)
  geom_point(data = gridpoint_station_table %>% 
               filter(!is.na(sta_id) & station_type == "primary"),
             aes(x = sta_lon, y = sta_lat, shape = "Primary Stations"),
             color = "red", size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
  
  # Plot secondary stations (yellow triangles)
  geom_point(data = gridpoint_station_table %>% 
               filter(!is.na(sta_id) & station_type == "secondary"),
             aes(x = sta_lon, y = sta_lat, shape = "Secondary Stations"),
             color = "gold", size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
  
  # Plot WOW stations (green diamonds)
  geom_point(data = unique_stations,
             aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, shape = "WOW Stations"),
             color = "darkgreen", size = 2.5, alpha = 0.3) +
  
  # Plot cities with size based on population rank
  geom_point(data = cities,
             aes(x = city_lon, y = city_lat, shape = "Cities", 
                 size = 26 - population_rank),  # Larger size for higher ranked cities
             color = "purple", alpha = 0.8) +
  
  # Set shapes for all point types
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Gridpoints" = 15,           # Square
                               "Primary Stations" = 16,       # Circle
                               "Secondary Stations" = 17,     # Triangle
                               "WOW Stations" = 18,          # Diamond
                               "Cities" = 19),               # Solid circle
                     name = "Type") +
  
  # Set size scale for cities
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(2, 6), guide = "none") +
  
  # Add gridpoint labels
  geom_text_repel(data = gridpoint_station_table,
                  aes(x = gp_lon, y = gp_lat, label = gridpoint),
                  size = 2.5,
                  color = "darkblue",
                  box.padding = 0.3,
                  point.padding = 0.2,
                  max.overlaps = 20) +
  
  # Add primary station labels
  geom_text_repel(data = gridpoint_station_table %>% 
                    filter(!is.na(sta_id) & station_type == "primary"),
                  aes(x = sta_lon, y = sta_lat, 
                      label = paste0("S", sta_id)),
                  size = 2,
                  color = "red",
                  nudge_y = 0.02,
                  box.padding = 0.2,
                  point.padding = 0.1,
                  max.overlaps = 20) +
  
  # Add secondary station labels
  geom_text_repel(data = gridpoint_station_table %>% 
                    filter(!is.na(sta_id) & station_type == "secondary"),
                  aes(x = sta_lon, y = sta_lat, 
                      label = paste0("S", sta_id)),
                  size = 2,
                  color = "gold",
                  nudge_y = -0.02,
                  box.padding = 0.2,
                  point.padding = 0.1,
                  max.overlaps = 20) +
  
  # Add city labels (for all cities)
  geom_text_repel(data = cities,
                  aes(x = city_lon, y = city_lat, label = city),
                  size = 2.5,
                  color = "purple",
                  fontface = "bold",
                  box.padding = 0.2,
                  point.padding = 0.2,
                  segment.size = 0.3,
                  segment.alpha = 0.5,
                  max.overlaps = 30,
                  force = 2) +
  
  # Titles and labels
  labs(title = "Weather Observation Network in the Netherlands",
       subtitle = "ECMWF Gridpoints, KNMI Stations (Primary/Secondary), WOW Stations, and Major Cities",
       x = "Longitude",
       y = "Latitude",
       caption = "Cities sized by population rank | WOW stations in green") +
  
  # Theme settings
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.box = "horizontal",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10),
        plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 8, face = "italic")) +
  
  # Set coordinate limits to focus on Netherlands
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(3.3, 7.3), ylim = c(50.7, 53.6))

# Display the map
gridpoint_station_map

```
#find 1 official station and 5 WOW stations that cloese to big cities
```{r}
library(geosphere)

create_city_station_connections <- function(date_str, n_wow_stations = 5) {
  
  cat("Creating city-station connection table for", date_str, "...\n")
  
  # 1. Get WOW stations for this date
  unique_stations <- get_unique_wow_stations(date_str)
  
  # 2. Check if there are enough WOW stations
  if (nrow(unique_stations) < n_wow_stations) {
    warning(paste("Insufficient WOW stations! Only", nrow(unique_stations), "stations available"))
    n_wow_stations <- nrow(unique_stations)
  }
  
  # 3. Create base city_connections (if not already exists)
  # Assumes city_connections already exists with city and nearest official station info
  # If not, need to create it first
  
  # 4. Add WOW station information to city_connections
  city_connections_with_wow <- city_connections %>%
    rowwise() %>%
    mutate(
      # Calculate distances to all WOW stations
      wow_distances = list(
        distHaversine(
          cbind(city_lon, city_lat), 
          cbind(unique_stations$Longitude, unique_stations$Latitude)
        ) / 1000  # Convert to kilometers
      ),
      # Find indices of nearest n stations
      nearest_wow_idx = list(order(wow_distances)[1:min(n_wow_stations, length(wow_distances))]),
      # Get distances of nearest stations
      nearest_wow_distances = list(wow_distances[nearest_wow_idx]),
      # Get coordinates of nearest stations
      nearest_wow_lat = list(unique_stations$Latitude[nearest_wow_idx]),
      nearest_wow_lon = list(unique_stations$Longitude[nearest_wow_idx]),
      # Get IDs of nearest stations
      nearest_wow_id = list(unique_stations$wow_id[nearest_wow_idx]),
      # Add date marker
      wow_data_date = date_str
    ) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    select(-wow_distances)  # Remove complete distance list
  
  # 5. Add useful summary information
  city_connections_with_wow <- city_connections_with_wow %>%
    mutate(
      # Distance to nearest WOW station
      closest_wow_distance = sapply(nearest_wow_distances, function(x) x[1]),
      # Whether WOW station is closer than official station
      wow_closer_than_official = closest_wow_distance < distance_km,
      # Distance improvement
      distance_improvement = distance_km - closest_wow_distance
    )
  
  # 6. Print summary statistics
  cat("\n===== Summary Statistics =====\n")
  cat("Date:", date_str, "\n")
  cat("Number of cities:", nrow(city_connections_with_wow), "\n")
  cat("Total WOW stations:", nrow(unique_stations), "\n")
  cat("WOW stations connected per city:", n_wow_stations, "\n")
  
  wow_better <- sum(city_connections_with_wow$wow_closer_than_official)
  cat("\nCities where WOW is closer than official:", wow_better, "/", nrow(city_connections_with_wow), "\n")
  cat("Average distance improvement:", 
      round(mean(city_connections_with_wow$distance_improvement[city_connections_with_wow$wow_closer_than_official]), 2), 
      "km\n")
  
  return(city_connections_with_wow)
}

#usage
city_connections_summer <- create_city_station_connections("2022-07-15")
```
#plot cities and its 5 closest WOW stations and 1 official station

```{r}
# Function to create city-station connection map
plot_city_station_connections <- function(city_connections_data, selected_cities = NULL) {
  
  # If specific cities are selected, filter the data
  if (!is.null(selected_cities)) {
    city_connections_data <- city_connections_data %>%
      filter(city %in% selected_cities)
  }
  
  # Prepare WOW stations data for plotting
  wow_stations_plot <- city_connections_data %>%
    select(city, nearest_wow_lat, nearest_wow_lon, nearest_wow_distances) %>%
    unnest(c(nearest_wow_lat, nearest_wow_lon, nearest_wow_distances)) %>%
    group_by(city) %>%
    mutate(
      wow_rank = row_number(),
      station_label = paste0("W", wow_rank)
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  # Create the map
  city_station_map <- ggplot() +
    # Add Netherlands boundary
    borders("world", regions = "Netherlands", 
            fill = NA, colour = "black", size = 0.8) +
    
    # Connection lines from cities to official stations
    geom_segment(data = city_connections_data,
                 aes(x = city_lon, y = city_lat, 
                     xend = station_lon, yend = station_lat),
                 color = "red", alpha = 0.3, linetype = "solid", size = 0.8) +
    
    # Connection lines from cities to WOW stations
    geom_segment(data = wow_stations_plot,
                 aes(x = city_connections_data$city_lon[match(city, city_connections_data$city)], 
                     y = city_connections_data$city_lat[match(city, city_connections_data$city)], 
                     xend = nearest_wow_lon, yend = nearest_wow_lat),
                 color = "darkgreen", alpha = 0.2, linetype = "dashed", size = 0.5) +
    
    # Plot official stations (red circles)
    geom_point(data = city_connections_data,
               aes(x = station_lon, y = station_lat, shape = "Official Stations"),
               color = "red", size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
    
    # Plot WOW stations (green diamonds)
    geom_point(data = wow_stations_plot,
               aes(x = nearest_wow_lon, y = nearest_wow_lat, shape = "WOW Stations"),
               color = "darkgreen", size = 2.5, alpha = 0.6) +
    
    # Plot cities (purple circles, sized by population)
    geom_point(data = city_connections_data,
               aes(x = city_lon, y = city_lat, shape = "Cities"),
               color = "purple", alpha = 0.8,size = 3.5) +
    
    # Set shapes
    scale_shape_manual(values = c("Official Stations" = 16,    # Circle
                                 "WOW Stations" = 18,          # Diamond
                                 "Cities" = 19),               # Solid circle
                       name = "Type") +
    
    # Set size scale for cities
    scale_size_continuous(range = c(3, 7), guide = "none") +
    
    # Add city labels
    geom_text_repel(data = city_connections_data,
                    aes(x = city_lon, y = city_lat, label = city),
                    size = 3,
                    color = "purple",
                    fontface = "bold",
                    box.padding = 0.3,
                    point.padding = 0.3,
                    max.overlaps = 30) +
    
    # Add official station labels
    geom_text_repel(data = city_connections_data,
                    aes(x = station_lon, y = station_lat, 
                        label = paste0("S", nearest_idx)),
                    size = 2,
                    color = "red",
                    box.padding = 0.2,
                    point.padding = 0.1,
                    max.overlaps = 20) +
    
    # Titles and labels
    labs(title = paste("City-Station Connections -", unique(city_connections_data$wow_data_date)),
         subtitle = "Cities with nearest Official Station (red) and 5 closest WOW Stations (green)",
         x = "Longitude",
         y = "Latitude",
         caption = "Solid lines: City to Official Station | Dashed lines: City to WOW Stations") +
    
    # Theme settings
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          legend.box = "horizontal",
          legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
          plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10),
          plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 8, face = "italic")) +
    
    # Set coordinate limits to focus on Netherlands
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(3.3, 7.3), ylim = c(50.7, 53.6))
  
  return(city_station_map)
}


#plot all
map_all <- plot_city_station_connections(city_connections_summer)
print(map_all)
```


#combine forecast with obs
```{r}
combine_forecast_obs_wow <- function(date_str, 
                               gridpoint_id, 
                               lead_time_hours = NULL,
                               aggregate_obs = "hourly",
                               include_metadata = TRUE) {
 
 # Get forecast data for the specified date and gridpoint
 forecast_data <- tidy_forecast_with_station_grid_location(date_str, gridpoint_id)
 
 ####### Import observation data for 10 days starting from date_str
 # Convert date_str to Date object
 start_date <- as.Date(date_str)
 
 # Initialize list to store observation data
 all_obs_data <- list()
 
 # Loop through 10 days
 for (i in 0:9) {
   current_date <- start_date + i
   current_date_str <- format(current_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
   
   tryCatch({
     # Import observation data for current date
     daily_obs <- import_knmi_data(current_date_str)
     all_obs_data[[current_date_str]] <- daily_obs
   })
 }
 
 # Combine all observation data
 if (length(all_obs_data) > 0) {
   obs_data <- bind_rows(all_obs_data)
 } else {
   warning("No observation data found for any date")
   return(forecast_data)
 }
 
 # Extract station_id from forecast data
 station_id <- unique(forecast_data$station_id)
 
 # Check if station_id exists
 if(is.na(station_id) || length(station_id) == 0) {
   warning("No station_id found for this gridpoint")
   return(forecast_data)
 }
 
 # Filter observation data for the specific station
 pattern <- paste0("^", station_id, "_")
 
 obs_filtered <- obs_data %>%
   filter(grepl(pattern, LOCATION)) %>%
   # Convert DTG to POSIXct to match forecast data format
   mutate(DTG = as.POSIXct(DTG, tz = "UTC"))
 
 # Check if any observations found
 if(nrow(obs_filtered) == 0) {
   warning(paste("No observations found for station", station_id))
   return(forecast_data)
 }
 
 # Ensure Valid_datetime is POSIXct
 forecast_data$Valid_datetime <- as.POSIXct(forecast_data$Valid_datetime, tz = "UTC")
 
 # Join observation data to forecast data
 combined_data <- forecast_data %>%
   left_join(
     obs_filtered,
     by = c("Valid_datetime" = "DTG"),
     suffix = c("_forecast", "_obs")
   )
 
 return(combined_data)
}

# Usage example
combined <- combine_forecast_obs_wow("2021-01-31 12:00:00", 605)
combined
```

#Combined visualisation funtion
```{r}
forecast_add_obs_line <- function(forecast_datetime, 
                                gridpoint_id, 
                                variable = "Temperature_C", 
                                members = 1:51,
                                temp_data = NULL,
                                dewp_data = NULL,
                                station_table = NULL) {
  
  # Variable mapping for observations
  var_mapping <- list(
    "Temperature_C" = "T_DRYB_10",
    "DewPoint_C" = "T_DEWP_10"
  )
  
  obs_var_name <- var_mapping[[variable]]
  if (is.null(obs_var_name)) {
    stop(paste("Invalid variable:", variable, 
               ". Valid options are:", paste(names(var_mapping), collapse = ", ")))
  }
  
  # Get the combined forecast and observation data
  combined <- combine_forecast_obs_wow(
    date_str = forecast_datetime, 
    gridpoint_id = gridpoint_id
  )
  
  if (is.null(combined) || nrow(combined) == 0) {
    warning(paste("No combined data found for", forecast_datetime, "at gridpoint", gridpoint_id))
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Filter for selected members
  member_names <- paste0("M", members)
  plot_data <- combined %>%
    filter(Member %in% member_names)
  
  # Create the plot
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = lead_time_hrs, y = .data[[variable]]))
  
  # Check number of members
  n_members <- length(members)
  
  # Add forecast lines
  if (n_members <= 10) {
    # Use colors that don't include red (reserved for observations)
    forecast_colors <- c("blue", "darkgreen", "purple", "orange", "brown", 
                        "pink", "gold", "cyan", "magenta", "gray")[1:n_members]
    p <- p + 
      geom_line(aes(color = Member, group = Member), 
                size = 0.8, alpha = 0.8) +
      scale_color_manual(
        name = "Ensemble",
        values = forecast_colors
      )
  } else {
    # Gray spaghetti plot for many members
    p <- p + 
      geom_line(aes(group = Member), 
                alpha = 0.3, color = "gray50", size = 0.3) +
      # Add ensemble mean
      stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", 
                   color = "black", size = 1, alpha = 0.9)
  }
  
  # Add observation line - check if observation data exists
  if (obs_var_name %in% names(plot_data) && !all(is.na(plot_data[[obs_var_name]]))) {
    p <- p + 
      geom_line(aes(y = .data[[obs_var_name]]), 
                color = "red", 
                size = 1.5, 
                alpha = 1,
                na.rm = TRUE) +
      geom_point(aes(y = .data[[obs_var_name]]), 
                 color = "darkred", 
                 size = 2, 
                 alpha = 0.8,
                 na.rm = TRUE)
  } else {
    warning(paste("No observation data found for variable:", obs_var_name))
  }
  
  # Add labels and theme
  station_info <- unique(plot_data$station_id)[1]
  p <- p +
    labs(
      title = paste("Forecast vs Observations -", 
                    format(unique(plot_data$Forecast_init_datetime)[1], "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC")),
      subtitle = paste("Gridpoint", gridpoint_id, "| Station:", station_info, 
                      "| Members:", n_members, "| Red: Observations"),
      x = "Lead Time (hours)",
      y = ifelse(variable == "Temperature_C", "Temperature (°C)", 
                 ifelse(variable == "DewPoint_C", "Dew Point (°C)", variable))
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, color = "gray40"),
      legend.position = if(n_members <= 10) "right" else "none",
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 240, 24)) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 240))
  
  # Add reference lines for days
  p <- p +
    geom_vline(xintercept = seq(24, 240, 24), alpha = 0.2, linetype = "dashed")
  
  return(p)
}

# Enhanced version with error statistics
forecast_add_obs_with_stats <- function(forecast_datetime, 
                                      gridpoint_id, 
                                      variable = "Temperature_C", 
                                      members = 1:51,
                                      show_error_band = TRUE) {
  
  # Variable mapping
  var_mapping <- list(
    "Temperature_C" = "T_DRYB_10",
    "DewPoint_C" = "T_DEWP_10"
  )
  
  obs_var_name <- var_mapping[[variable]]
  
  # Get combined data
  combined <- combine_forecast_obs_wow(forecast_datetime, gridpoint_id)
  
  if (is.null(combined) || nrow(combined) == 0) {
    stop("No data found")
  }
  
  # Filter members
  member_names <- paste0("M", members)
  plot_data <- combined %>%
    filter(Member %in% member_names)
  
  # Calculate ensemble statistics
  ensemble_stats <- plot_data %>%
    group_by(lead_time_hrs) %>%
    summarise(
      ensemble_mean = mean(.data[[variable]], na.rm = TRUE),
      ensemble_median = median(.data[[variable]], na.rm = TRUE),
      ensemble_q25 = quantile(.data[[variable]], 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
      ensemble_q75 = quantile(.data[[variable]], 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
      ensemble_q10 = quantile(.data[[variable]], 0.1, na.rm = TRUE),
      ensemble_q90 = quantile(.data[[variable]], 0.9, na.rm = TRUE),
      obs_value = first(.data[[obs_var_name]]),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  # Create plot
  p <- ggplot()
  
  # Add error bands if requested
  if (show_error_band) {
    p <- p +
      geom_ribbon(data = ensemble_stats,
                  aes(x = lead_time_hrs, ymin = ensemble_q10, ymax = ensemble_q90),
                  fill = "lightblue", alpha = 0.3) +
      geom_ribbon(data = ensemble_stats,
                  aes(x = lead_time_hrs, ymin = ensemble_q25, ymax = ensemble_q75),
                  fill = "blue", alpha = 0.3)
  }
  
  # Add individual members if not too many
  if (length(members) <= 20) {
    p <- p +
      geom_line(data = plot_data,
                aes(x = lead_time_hrs, y = .data[[variable]], group = Member),
                alpha = 0.3, color = "gray50", size = 0.3)
  }
  
  # Add ensemble mean and median
  p <- p +
    geom_line(data = ensemble_stats,
              aes(x = lead_time_hrs, y = ensemble_mean),
              color = "blue", size = 1.2) +
    geom_line(data = ensemble_stats,
              aes(x = lead_time_hrs, y = ensemble_median),
              color = "darkblue", size = 1, linetype = "dashed")
  
  # Add observations
  p <- p +
    geom_line(data = ensemble_stats,
              aes(x = lead_time_hrs, y = obs_value),
              color = "red", size = 1.5, na.rm = TRUE) +
    geom_point(data = ensemble_stats,
               aes(x = lead_time_hrs, y = obs_value),
               color = "darkred", size = 2, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Labels and theme
  p <- p +
    labs(
      title = paste("Forecast vs Observations -", 
                    format(as.POSIXct(forecast_datetime), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC")),
      subtitle = paste("Blue: Ensemble mean/median | Red: Observations | Shading: 10-90% & 25-75% intervals"),
      x = "Lead Time (hours)",
      y = ifelse(variable == "Temperature_C", "Temperature (°C)", variable)
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 240, 24))
  
  return(p)
}

# Usage examples:
# Basic plot
p1 <- forecast_add_obs_line(
  forecast_datetime = "2021-02-02 00:00:00",
  gridpoint_id = 605
)
print(p1)

# With error statistics
p2 <- forecast_add_obs_with_stats(
  forecast_datetime = "2021-02-01 00:00:00",
  gridpoint_id = 605,
  variable = "Temperature_C",
  show_error_band = TRUE
)
print(p2)


```

###combine obs with WOW
```{r}

```
